content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 3, 1987


Mr. Phil Anderson
P.O. Box 714
Walnut, CA  91788-0714

RE:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I 87-276

Dear Mr. Anderson:

     The Commission is in receipt of your letter dated
October 26, 1987, and a copy of a mass mailing which was sent to
the voters on October 26, 1987, supporting your candidacy in the
November 3, 1987 election.1/

     Government Code Section 843052/ was amended effective
October 1, 1978, by Chapter 1408 of the Statutes of 1978, to
delete the requirement that a copy of all mass mailings be sent
to the Fair Political Practices Commission.

     The 1987 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act," Part IX (copy enclosed),
provides recordkeeping guidelines for candidates and committee
treasurers.  Subsection 3 on page 41 of the manual sets out the
types of documents which require retention. Here it states that
"A copy of each mass mailing made by a candidate or committee
should also be kept."  You must retain a copy of all mass
mailings for a period of four years.  (Section 84104; Regulation
18401 copy enclosed.)


_____________________
1/Your letter states only a general question.  Therefore,
we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant
to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).  Informal assistance does
not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an
opinion or formal written advice.  (Section 84113; Regulation
18329(c)(3).)

2/All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2
California Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq.  All
references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the
California Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Mr. Phil Anderson
Page 2


     Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional
questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                 [illegible]

                    By:    Mary Ann Kvasager
                           Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:kmt

FPO
PO Box 1467
1230 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95807

10-26-87

Nov 2 4:20 PM 87

Mr. Anderson-to-elect of
Roncano United School District

I can't locate the center, however, I think it am & notify
you of these Mailing Central

A attached will be mailed today-but that News Word is
Vacuum adm write to cluttered.

[signature] Pat Cody Clattner
MAIL AND ——→ PO Box 791
Walnut, Calif
91788-0714

End-2-March-1988!

"ANDY" gram

PHIL "ANDY" ANDERSON 18063 Jeannie Dr., La Puente, CA 91744 (818) 964-5252

Dear Voters,

Someone once said, "Campaign Promises are made to be broken."
I don't happen to share that point of view, and I don't think you do either.

You have elected me to serve our district since 1969, and once again I am
asking you for your vote on NOVEMBER 3, 1987.

My record has been established. It is a Proven record of performance.

Be sure to vote, and take a friend. Every vote counts.

Thank you in advance

[Phil "Andy" Anderson signature]

A copy of my official candidate statement is enclosed in case you lost or
mislaced yours.

Let's Keep Rowland Unified

Vote On November 3rd, 1987

VOTE FOR "ANDY"

VOTE FOR "ANDY"

Phil "Andy" Anderson 18063 Jeannie Dr. La Puente Ca 91744 (818) 964-5252

Reprinted for you is a copy of my Candidate Statement - in case you lost or misplaced your Sample Ballot.

STATEMENT OF: PHIL "ANDY" ANDERSON
CANDIDATE FOR GOVERNING BOARD MEMBER
ROWLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

You the voters have honored me by re-electing me to office on the Rowland Unified School Board since 1969. I am pleased to report to you that our school district is in excellent condition. Test scores are at the highest level ever. All three elementary schools are identified as distinguished California schools. All three high schools are big winners in the Cash for CAP program enabling them to use the money for staff training, a library addition and other program improvements. The District offers programs ranging from Kindergarten (PREPPIE) to high school that serve the unique needs of our students. Adult education classes are offering opportunities for adults to re-train for new jobs, obtain high school diplomas, and learn our language and study about our political system to become citizens. As your elected representative, I have demanded efficient management and have fought for student safety through substance abuse training, bussing, and strong discipline policies.

Your vote will enable me to continue representing both parent and non-parent taxpayers while striving for maximum use of our tax dollar. Youth is our most important asset and my experience as a board member will enable me to continue the development of Rowland's total educational system.

VOTE FOR ANDY
NOVEMBER 3, 1987
ROWLAND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

LET'S KEEP ROWLAND UNIFIED - VOTE ON NOVEMBER 3rd, 1987

VOTE FOR "ANDY"
